title: Trust the runner, not the model's narration
description: Tool results and observed state are ground truth; the model's claim that it 'completed' a step is not.
body: Use when the agent reports progress or success. Do not mark a step done because the model's text says it finished, and avoid feeding the model's self-report back as confirmed fact. Actions: derive completion from actual tool results and state changes the runner observed, reconcile the model's claim against those results, and surface any divergence. Validation signals include a test where the model claims success but the tool failed and the runner records failure, not success. Risk boundary: acting on a false success claim (e.g. reporting a deploy done that never ran) is a blocking finding; gate completion on observed evidence.
